verdict_reasoning: HSPA1A (HSP70) is a canonical cytosolic chaperone with no membrane anchor in its baseline state. However, it has well-documented surface display under stress and pathological conditions: (1) Heat shock and cellular stress induce translocation of HSP70 to the outer leaflet, enabling recognition by pattern-recognition receptors and immune activation. (2) Tumor cells constitutively display HSP70 as an ecto-form on the cancer-cell surface, facilitating NK recognition and immune targeting. (3) Cell-death-induced display during apoptosis and necroptosis externalizes cytosolic HSP70 to the PM outer face. (4) HSP70-based therapeutic antibodies and CAR-T programs target HSP70 on the surface of stress-exposed and tumor cells, confirming accessible ecto-pools. The baseline biology is cytosolic, but multiple robust, documented, and therapeutically-relevant non-baseline contexts move HSP70 to the outer leaflet, qualifying it as contextual surface-accessible.
